name: Phind
what_it_does: AI-powered search engine optimized for developers, with an API for technical question answering and code generation with web-grounded citations.
use_cases: Answering technical questions with web-searched, cited responses inside an agent workflow
Searching for code examples and library usage patterns at query time
Generating code snippets with explanations grounded in current documentation
Augmenting an agent with real-time developer knowledge retrieval
Finding solutions to error messages and stack traces with context-aware answers
not_for: General-purpose web search beyond the developer/technical domain
High-volume batch processing of searches due to rate limits on free tier
best_when: Best when an agent needs real-time, web-grounded answers to technical and developer questions with code examples and source citations.
avoid_when: Avoid when you need a public, stable API with guaranteed uptime SLA — Phind's API is offered more as a developer preview than a production service tier.

auth.notes: API key passed as Bearer token in Authorization header. Simple curl-compatible authentication.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Unlimited basic searches on free tier; API access requires Pro plan
pricing.paid_tiers: Phind Pro at $20/month includes API access and advanced model access
pricing.notes: Free web UI is unlimited; API access for programmatic use requires Pro subscription.

performance.rate_limits: Pro tier: rate limits not publicly documented; estimated at tens of requests per minute

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Limited public API documentation; error responses not comprehensively documented.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Search queries are read-only and naturally idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: API is not prominently documented and may change without notice
Response latency is high (1-5s) due to real-time web search; agents must handle long waits
No streaming support in basic API tier; full response must be awaited

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. No published security certifications. Proprietary service with standard web security practices.
